sections.paragraph:
ἀμέμπτως adv. fr. ἄμεμπτος (Aeschyl. et al.; ins, pap; Esth 3:13d; Philo, Migr. Abr. 129; Just., D. 8, 3) used esp. in the Gr-Rom. world of people of extraordinary civic consciousness blamelessly (w. ὁσίως and δικαίως) γενηθῆναι comport oneself blamelessly 1 Th 2:10; ἀ. τηρεῖσθαι be kept blameless 5:23; ἀναστρέφεσθαι ἀ. conduct oneself blamelessly 1 Cl 63:3; δουλεύειν τῷ κυρίῳ ἀ. Hv 4, 2, 5; δουλεύειν τῷ πνεύματι ἀ. Hs 5, 6, 7 (text restored). λειτουργεῖν ἀ. (UPZ 20, 62 [163 b.c.]) 1 Cl 44:3; προσφέρειν ἀ. 44:4; μετάγειν ἐκ τῆς ἀ. αὐτοῖς τετιμημένης λειτουργίας remove them from the office which they filled blamelessly 44:6 (on the text see Knopf, Hdb.). 1 Th 3:13 v.l.—Danker, Benefactor 354f; New Docs 4, 141 (ins and pap).—DELG s.v. μέμφομαι.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

  1. fr. = from

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

esp. esp. = especially

Gr-Rom. Gr-Rom. = Greco-Roman (gener. in contrast to Israelite/Christian tradition)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

Hs Hs = Similitudes

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Danker, Benefactor Danker, Benefactor = FD., Benefactor: Epigraphic Study—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 53
